commit.message:
fix(web): tone down Search Volume card chart background

Changed bar chart SVG from orange (#f97316) at 4-8% opacity to neutral
gray (#a1a1aa) at 3-5% opacity. Donut ring also toned down. Much subtler
background that doesn't compete with the card content.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add fcntl.h + unistd.h for Linux POSIX compat (open/read/close)

Linux CI failed: ::open and O_RDONLY undeclared without <fcntl.h>/<unistd.h>
MacOS gets these transitively via BSD headers; Linux requires explicit include.
Guarded with #ifndef _WIN32 for Windows compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* Add integration tests and fix bugs: OTA Enumerator indexing, MaintenanceRecord photo validation for system records

- Add 7 new integration test files covering blockchain, OTA, gateway telemetry, audit logs, TinyML, account security, reports/dashboard/settings, provisioning/controllers
- Fix bug: OtaTransmissionWorker packages Enumerator not supporting [] indexing (convert to array)

Co-authored-by: Alexey-Lukin <619149+Alexey-Lukin@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix 15 test failures, 8 production bugs, and lint issues; coverage reaches 84.9% integration / 92.8% overall

Bug fixes discovered during test writing:
- TinyMlModel: add missing binary_sha256 method (OTA for TinyML was broken)
- BioContractFirmware: add missing deployment_count method (firmware index 500)
- FirmwaresController: permit bytecode_payload in strong params
- ProvisioningController: fix gateway provisioning calling .did on Gateway (use .uid)
- TelemetryController: fix params[:tree_id]/[:gateway_id] → params[:id] (route mismatch)
- ActuatorsController: fix actuator_commands → commands (wrong association name)
- NaasContract: add missing active_threats? method
- ContractsController: fix ews_alerts.active → .unresolved, fix blockchain_transactions association
- User: add missing stream_access token generator

Test infrastructure fixes:
- Factory sequences start at 900_000 to avoid seed data collisions
- Fix all rubocop lint offenses in spec/integration/

commit.message:
fix: load SSH env-secrets and restore zsh Alt keybindings (#118)

* fix: load codespace env-secrets in zsh and bash SSH sessions

When connecting to a codespace via SSH, environment variables from
/workspaces/.codespaces/shared/.env-secrets were not loaded for zsh
and bash shells. This caused missing tokens, PATH entries, and other
codespace-injected env vars.

Add codespace-env blocks (mirroring the existing codespaces.fish fix)
to ~/.zshenv, ~/.bashrc, and ~/.bashenv that:
- Read and base64-decode each entry from .env-secrets
- Merge PATH by appending missing entries (preserving shell-managed priority)
- Export all other variables directly
- Only activate during SSH sessions (SSH_CONNECTION check)
- Use marker comments for idempotent re-runs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add zsh Alt keybindings in vi insert mode

Add the missing Alt+Backspace, Alt+D, Alt+Left/Right, Alt+B, and Alt+F bindings in the vi insert keymap so the generated zsh config preserves expected word-editing shortcuts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: parallelize canvas task polling + unlimited SSE reconnect

Root cause: sequential polling causes one slow query to block detection
of other tasks' completion. SSE max 5 reconnects causes permanent
event channel loss.

Changes:
- WorkflowCanvas: parallelize non-decomposition task status queries
  with Promise.allSettled (one slow query no longer blocks others)
- useTaskProgress: remove 5-attempt reconnect limit, use exponential
  backoff up to 60s (SSE channel stays alive for entire session)
- task_dispatcher: add active Kling task count diagnostic logging
  to help diagnose potential account-level concurrency limits

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: domain verification UX — console noise, DNS instructions, error handling

- CustomDomainService.detect(): guard against both siteUrl AND apiUrl hostnames
  so /public/contest-by-domain is never called on the main ctf.rootaccess.live
  site (eliminates spurious 404 in browser console on every page load)

- contest-manager loadDomainStatus(): reconstruct DNS instructions (CNAME +
  TXT records) from persisted domain status when domain is set but unverified
  and domainSetResponse is null — prevents instructions disappearing on reload

- contest-manager saveDomain(): strip http(s):// prefix from domain input
  before submitting; show warning toast so user knows it was auto-corrected

- GetDomainStatus handler: return 500 for DB/internal errors, only 404 for
  actual sql.ErrNoRows — prevents cold-start Lambda DB failures appearing as
  404 in the browser

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(web): fix marquee animation and make label text 2 lines on mobile - Moved animation from CSS class to inline style to prevent Tailwind 4 purging - Removed whitespace-nowrap, added max-w-[180px] on mobile for natural 2-line wrap - Added
on mobile between '2,000+' and 'companies of all sizes' Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Increase encryption key generation from 128-bit to 256-bit (#255844)

Generated encryption keys were 128-bit (16 bytes → 32 hex chars), which
falls short of current security standards. Keys should be 256-bit to
align with AES-256 requirements.

## Changes

- **`src/cli/encryption_keys/encryption_config.js`**: Updated
`_generateEncryptionKey()` to use `crypto.randomBytes(32)` instead of
`crypto.randomBytes(16)`, producing 64-character hex strings (256-bit
keys)

```js
// Before
return crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('hex');  // 128-bit, 32 hex chars

// After
return crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex');  // 256-bit, 64 hex chars
```

This affects all three generated keys:
`xpack.encryptedSavedObjects.encryptionKey`,
`xpack.reporting.encryptionKey`, and `xpack.security.encryptionKey`.
Existing configured keys are unaffected — only newly generated keys will
use the increased length.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
>

> 
> <issue_title>Update encryption key strength to 256 bits (32 chars) in
encryption_config.js</issue_title>
> <issue_description>Update the key strength to 256 bits (32 characters)
for cryptographic operations to improve security.
> 
> **Context:**
> In `src/cli/encryption_keys/encryption_config.js` (see
[source](https://github.com/elastic/kibana/blob/89acfef87079edda2780454863c20756f07e2b7d/src/cli/encryption_keys/encryption_config.js#L49)),
the current key size appears insufficient for modern security
guarantees. The implementation should be updated so that generated or
validated keys are 256 bits (32 characters) in length.
> 
> - Review the key generation (`crypto.randomBytes(16)`) and update to
`crypto.randomBytes(32)` or equivalent.
> - Amend validation logic to require 32-character keys.
> - Update related documentation, comments, and any usage examples to
reflect the new requirement.
> 
> **Acceptance criteria:**
> - All locations in the file require and enforce a minimum length of 32
characters for encryption keys.
> - Documentation and code comments are up-to-date with the new key
size.</issue_description>

Add configurable verbose logging and fix UNC path resolution

- Add VerboseLogging config option with UI checkbox in Settings
- Add Debug log level that only writes when verbose logging is enabled
- Add detailed debug logging throughout the pipeline:
  - ShortcutResolver: logs resolved target or reason for failure
  - ExclusionEngine: logs each rule comparison result
  - RecentFileCleaner: logs match/no-match decisions per file
  - RecentFileWatcher: logs detected file events
- Add 'Open Log File' button in Settings and tray context menu
- Show verbose logging status in tray context menu
- Fix critical bug: CommonPathSuffixOffset in .lnk parser was read from
  wrong offset (0x14 instead of 0x18) when hasLocalBasePath=false,
  causing all UNC/network path shortcuts to resolve incorrectly
- Add Unicode CommonPathSuffix support for network paths

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(orleans): R5 final audit — cap SettingsBroadcast registrations, add [PreferLocalPlacement] to singleton orchestrators, replace cross-grain validation throws with log-and-return

- SettingsBroadcastGrain: Add MaxRegisteredAssets=200 hard cap on
  _registeredAssets ConcurrentDictionary to prevent unbounded growth
  from buggy clients, API replays, or activation storms

- SchedulerGrain + PortfolioMakerGrain: Add [PreferLocalPlacement] to
  singleton orchestrator grains with persisted state and reminders.
  Default random placement caused unnecessary cross-silo latency on
  every reminder tick from 22+ asset grains

- MLTrainingGrain.SubmitGradientsAsync: Replace ArgumentException /
  ArgumentOutOfRangeException throws with LogWarning + early return.
  Raw throws serialized cross-silo, deserialized by caller, then caught
  generically with a vague log. Now logs the specific validation failure
  locally and returns silently, avoiding expensive exception round-trips

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(raster): extract damage computation helpers

Extract per-command-type damage calculation logic from ComputeDamageForCommand
into dedicated helper functions:
- damageFillRect
- damageFillRoundedRect

commit.message:
Security: Add authorization check to claude.yml workflow (#906)

## Summary

This PR addresses a critical security vulnerability in the `claude.yml`
workflow that allowed any external user to trigger AI-powered actions
with write access to the repository.

## Problem

The `claude.yml` workflow could be triggered by **any user** (including
external contributors) by including `@claude` in:
- Issue comments
- PR review comments
- PR reviews
- Issue titles/bodies

The workflow has extensive permissions including:
- `contents: write` - Can modify repository code
- `pull-requests: write` - Can create/modify PRs
- `issues: write` - Can create/modify issues
- `actions: write` - Can trigger other workflows

## Solution

Added an authorization check step that:
1. ✅ **Verifies user authorization** before running any workflow steps
2. ✅ **Allows only**:
   - Organization members (`MEMBER`)
   - Organization owners (`OWNER`)
   - Repository collaborators (`COLLABORATOR`)
3. ✅ **Blocks all other users** (contributors, first-timers, etc.)
4. ✅ **Adds thumbs-down reaction** (👎) to unauthorized comments/issues
5. ✅ **Logs clear failure messages** for audit purposes
6. ✅ **Bypasses check** for manual `workflow_dispatch` triggers

## Changes

- Added `Check Authorization` step at the beginning of the workflow
- Step runs before checkout to prevent unnecessary actions
- Uses `gh api` to add reaction icons for user feedback
- Exits with error code 1 to fail the workflow for unauthorized users

## Testing

Manually tested authorization logic:
- ✅ MEMBER/OWNER/COLLABORATOR users pass authorization
- ✅ Other users fail with clear error message
- ✅ Unauthorized attempts get thumbs-down reaction

## Security Impact

This fix prevents:
- Unauthorized code modifications
- Unauthorized PR/issue creation
- Abuse of `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` credits
- Malicious prompt injection attacks
- Information extraction via AI prompts

---

**Security Severity:** Critical
**Impact:** Prevents external users from executing actions with write
access to the repository

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add H146-H148 security heuristics (SBO, NPD, memcpy bounds)

H146: Stack Buffer Overflow GetValues — detects numeric array tags
where GetSize() exceeds kMaxSafeChannels (16), preventing stack
buffer overflow when GetValues() writes into fixed-size buffers.
CWE-121. Covers PoCs #551, #618, #649, #625, #624, #537.

H147: Null Pointer After Tag Read — validates post-Read() state
of tags that leave internal pointers null on malformed data
(Utf16Text, TextDescription, MPE elements, tag table entries).
CWE-476. Covers PoCs #553, #560, #484, #485, #507, #633.

H148: Memory Copy Bounds Overlap — analyzes MPE element chains
for channel count oscillation causing memcpy src/dst overlap in
Apply() ping-pong buffers. Validates NamedColor2 deviceCoords <= 15.
CWE-119. Covers PoC #577.

Heuristic count: 145 → 148. uniqueCVEs: 85 → 87.
All 217 tests pass. 0 ASAN/UBSAN errors on diverse profiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix QuickSight ingestion collapsing multiple datasets on the same datasource into a single DataModel (#26317)

* Initial plan

* fix: use dataset_id instead of datasource_id for QuickSight DataModel identity

When multiple QuickSight datasets share the same datasource, the DataModel
entity was identified by the datasource ID, causing all datasets to collapse
into a single DataModel. This fix changes the identity to use the dataset ID,
ensuring each dataset creates a separate DataModel entity with its own columns.

commit.message:
fix: correct UV normalisation condition in phi_ubt_derivation.tex §1(c) (#381)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct UV normalisation in subsection 1(c) of phi_ubt_derivation.tex

The displayed equation in subsection 1(c) read "1 = 3/(2πR_ψ²) + 1/π²"
with text claiming "α = 1/(4π)" normalization. However, the formula
R_ψ^SC = √(3π/(8π³−2)) ≈ 0.196 that follows is correct only for the
"4π = ..." equation (i.e. α = 1 normalization). Fix the text and
displayed equation to consistently state α = 1 (strong coupling at the
UBT cutoff scale, bracket = 4π), matching the problem statement spec
"Φ × C5 × N_eff + spectral = 4π".

Co-authored-by: DavJ <12070775+DavJ@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: DavJ <12070775+DavJ@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: switch BBB source from 720p to 1080p (zip archive)

The Blender Foundation no longer hosts a standalone 720p MP4; all
downloads are 1080p+ zip archives.  Switch to
bbb_sunflower_1080p_30fps_normal.mp4.zip (~275 MiB) and add a zip
extraction step before the FFmpeg processing.  Update all filename
references (bbb_720p_30s.mkv → bbb_1080p_30s.mkv), the synthetic
fallback task description, and the documentation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix patch_size for pixel-space training (2 → 16)

In latent space, patch_size=2 on 128×128 inputs gave 4,096 tokens.
After switching to pixel-space (1024×1024), that same patch_size=2
produced 262,144 tokens — a 64× increase that caused ~10× wall-clock
slowdown (O(n²) attention).

patch_size=16 in pixel space covers the same 16×16 real-pixel area
as patch_size=2 did in 8× downsampled VAE latent space, restoring
the original 4,096 token sequence length at full resolution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add security policy (#4)

Signed-off-by: Luke Carr <me+oss@carr.sh>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard inspector against render flooding

- Batch log stream entries: onEntry now buffers logs and flushes
  at most every 100ms instead of calling StateHasChanged per entry
- Add 10s timeout to ConnectAsync so a hung agent can't block forever
- Fix recursive StateHasChanged in ExpandAllDescendants/CollapseAllDescendants:
  split into Core methods that traverse without rendering, call
  StateHasChanged once at the end
- Clear pending log buffer on ClearAllLogs and Dispose

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle track vs context URIs in search play actions

PlayTrack only works with spotify:track: URIs. Playlists, albums,
and artists use context_uri via PlayContext instead. Enter now plays
the item (queue for tracks, context play for playlists/albums).
P (Shift+P) does play-now for tracks, shuffle play for contexts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite AdvancedSearchField with standard autocomplete pattern

- useDebouncedValue hook for proper debounce (500ms default)
- Suggestions fetch via useEffect, never block input
- Plain div listbox dropdown, no cmdk dependency
- Keyboard navigation (Arrow keys, Enter, Escape)
- ARIA combobox attributes for accessibility
- onMouseDown+preventDefault keeps focus on input
- Race-condition safe with useEffect cleanup

fix(vs-ext): add missing closing braces and remove DialogPage from net8.0 project

EvalRangeCommand.cs and CellNavigationCommands.cs were missing the
closing } for their class declarations (CS1513 on CI windows build).

SageFsOptions.cs used DialogPage from Microsoft.VisualStudio.Shell
which is not available in the VS Extensibility SDK (net8.0) project.
Replaced with plain POCO — settings persistence will be wired via
the VS Ext SDK settings API in a follow-up sprint.
